expectations.text: Does NOT flag 'transparent' as a hardcoded color violation
expectations.evidence: Section A: 'Both are acceptable — transparent is a CSS-native keyword that represents a deliberate design decision, not a missing token.'

expectations.text: Does NOT flag 'inherit' as a hardcoded value violation
expectations.evidence: Section B: 'This is acceptable — it is a deliberate cascade decision.'

expectations.text: Does NOT flag 'currentColor' as a hardcoded color violation
expectations.evidence: Section C: 'This is a legitimate CSS pattern for maintaining color coherence.'

expectations.text: If any actual hardcoded values exist alongside these keywords, those ARE correctly flagged
expectations.evidence: Flags padding:0 and border:none as 'Low severity' — these are borderline but shows the system would catch actual violations.

expectations.text: Explicitly acknowledges transparent, inherit, and currentColor as legitimate CSS values
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Edge-Case Keywords Requiring Judgment' section with detailed per-keyword assessment.

expectations.text: BLOCKING vs WARNING severity applied correctly for any real violations found
expectations.evidence: Uses 'Low/None' severity scale instead of BLOCKING/WARNING/SERIOUS. No awareness of the token audit severity framework.

expectations.text: Output follows the structured audit report format
expectations.evidence: Uses its own format (Summary, Flagged Hardcoded Values, Edge-Case Keywords, Compliance Scorecard, Recommendations) — not the TOKEN AUDIT template from the skill.

expectations.text: Overall audit result reflects that this component has good token compliance

eval_feedback.overall: Surprisingly strong without-skill performance on the edge case — correctly handled all CSS keywords. Failed on severity framework and output template, which are the skill-specific differentiators. Also flagged layout properties (display, flex-shrink, cursor) as 'hardcoded values' which shows lack of focus on what actually matters for token compliance.
